contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: StarCi is built by one person — your question reaches the person who makes it. Pick the fastest route below, or send a message.
contact.responseTime: We usually reply within 24 hours (support hours Mon–Fri, 9:00–18:00).

form.title: Send a message
form.name: Full name
form.successBody: Thanks. We'll get back to you by email as soon as we can.
form.errorTitle: Couldn't send your message. Please try again.

faq.title: Frequently asked
faq.q1: How soon will I get a reply?
faq.a1: Usually within 24 hours, during support hours Mon–Fri 9:00–18:00.
faq.q2: I can't log in or forgot my password?
faq.a2: Use "Forgot password" on the sign-in screen to reset it. If you're still stuck, send the form with your account email.
faq.a3: Send the form with the "Course / account support" reason and your transaction id, and we'll look into it.
faq.q4: Want to partner or hire StarCi learners?
faq.a4: Pick the "Partnership / hiring" reason when sending the form, or message the founder directly on LinkedIn.
faq.q5: Questions about the courses, or whether StarCi is right for you?
faq.a5: See the FAQ on the home page — it covers the roadmap, who it's for, and how learning works in detail.
faq.landingLink: See course FAQ on the home page

home.intro: IT courses are everywhere online, but content that is concise and focuses on practical thinking is rare.
home.intro2: StarCi Academy was founded to change that.
home.intro3: We focus on building programming thinking - from Fullstack, DevOps, Security to Solution Architect - with a clear and practical roadmap.

diff.title: What makes StarCi Academy different?
diff.des1: StarCi Academy focuses on the essence of the IT industry. We don't just teach knowledge, but also train standard programming thinking and system design skills - core competencies that help you develop sustainably in the AI era.
diff.des3: Technology changes constantly and knowledge is everywhere. We focus on teaching the fundamentals, keywords, and thinking skills so that after the course, you can research, read docs, and expand on any stack you want.
diff.des5: StarCi Academy operates on a lean model, eliminating unnecessary costs to keep tuition affordable. Our goal is to create a high-quality learning environment that anyone serious about the profession can join.
diff.des6: We teach you to build truly valuable projects and provide long-term support throughout your career development.

hero.headline: Knowledge & Thinking to conquer every <accent>interview</accent>
hero.subline:
Back then, interns could start with small tasks: fixing UI, writing CRUD APIs, and boilerplate code.
In the AI era, those tasks are already solved in seconds.
Companies pay top salaries for practical Thinking & Knowledge — the part AI still cannot replace.
hero.diagramCaption: Spot where it breaks. Design systems that won’t break.
hero.ctaFounder: Meet the founder of StarCi Academy

stats.title: What StarCi Academy has
stats.intro: Data is updated in real time from the system.

courses.title: Three tracks. One industry-standard mindset.
courses.intro: Each track is built with strong structure and differs by level, but they share one disciplined mindset: we do not mislead learners for money. Every piece of content is carefully crafted.

fullstack.title: Fullstack
fullstack.desc: APIs · auth · caching · realtime · payments, plus frontends that survive real traffic.

systemDesign.title: System Design
systemDesign.desc: Sharding · replication · consensus · idempotency — design for failure.

devops.title: DevOps & Cloud
devops.desc: CI/CD · Docker · Kubernetes · multi-cloud · observability · on-call.

roadmap.title: Three mastery tracks — learn with structure
roadmap.intro: Each track runs foundation → application with real capstone projects. Follow in order, or pick your track.

fullstack.label: Fullstack
fullstack.caption: Build the whole product — backend to frontend

systems.title: Real systems you build with your own hands
systems.intro: Every capstone is a real production system — not a to-do app.

founder.title: The blunt truth about tech
founder.name: Nguyễn Văn Tự Cường
founder.titles: CTO @ Nivo Technology · CTO @ TEDO Technology
founder.quote: Relax boss — I can still fix the bug.
founder.body1: I teach exactly what I ship in production every day — not slides, real systems at CTO scale.
founder.body2: Everything here is built in the open — the platform, the curriculum, the challenges. Read the engineering notes and judge the work for yourself.
founder.thesis: Plenty of places teach coding to make you feel good and earn a certificate fast. StarCi Academy is different — I only tell the blunt truths about this industry and what real systems actually demand.
founder.truth1: Big-project experience does not guarantee interview success.
founder.fix1: Many people ship large projects daily, yet still cannot draw a clear distributed architecture in interviews. I teach you to reason about architecture and defend it clearly.
founder.truth2: Memorizing Microservices theory still gets you rejected when you're pushed on fundamentals.
founder.fix2: Strong interviewers do not ask definitions. They drill into data consistency, network failures, and single points of failure. I train you to understand the root so you can hold your ground.
founder.truth3: Using template answers from blogs is the fastest way to get exposed in interviews.
founder.fix3: Textbook architectures often collapse against real business problems. I teach you independent thinking so you can handle any variation.
founder.truth4: The market has no room left for fluff — no one pays Senior salary for keyword name-dropping.
founder.fix4: Companies are tightening standards hard. I train you in real architecture capability so you can pass tough screening rounds with confidence.

recruiter.title: Hire engineers who've proven it
recruiter.intro: Learners who finished the work and opted in to be hired — vetted by what they built, not by a résumé.
recruiter.count: {count}+ engineers open to work right now

engineer.title: For users
engineer.body: You build a portfolio from real, production-grade projects, and I take it straight to hiring teams. They call you for what you can do, not for a polished CV.

enterprise.title: For companies
enterprise.body: Skip inflated CVs. Hire people with real systems-thinking depth.

faq.title: Questions, answered honestly
faq.q1: Is this another bootcamp promising a high salary in 3 months?
faq.a1: No. I don't sell a salary number. I train architectural thinking and prove it with graded challenges — the rest is on you and the market.
faq.q2: Will AI make these skills obsolete?
faq.a2: The opposite. AI writes CRUD already. What it can't do is own trade-offs, design for scale and defend decisions — which is exactly what I train.
faq.q3: I'm a beginner. Is this for me?
faq.a3: If you can already write basic code and want to level up to designing systems, yes. If you've never coded, start with fundamentals first.
faq.q4: How does the AI grading actually work?
faq.a4: Each challenge is scored against production-grade criteria with written feedback. You see exactly why you passed or what to fix.
faq.a5: Opt in as open-to-work and your graded work becomes visible to hiring partners browsing the talent directory. No guarantees — just real signal.

closing.title: No more learning just to know. Time to build real systems.
closing.subtitle: Train to do real work, with enough grit to clear every technical interview in the language you already use.

learnLoop.title: Learn with my method.
learnLoop.intro: In interviews, what matters is not years of experience, but your problem-solving mindset. I systematized the industry's core knowledge into this passion project and built an effective learning method so more people can access it and break through.

grade.title: AI-graded challenge
grade.desc: Write code, scored on correctness & design.

capstone.title: Capstone
capstone.desc: Ship a production-grade system, not a toy.

rank.title: Leaderboard
rank.desc: Compare solutions and climb the ranks.

treasure.title: Learn to solve real problems, not to memorize answers.
treasure.intro: From backend to infrastructure, you train end-to-end problem-solving mindset — the thing that decides whether you survive on large projects.
treasure.interconnect: Idempotency, learned in System Design — reused in Payment, Webhooks (Fullstack).
treasure.beaconTap: Auto-reshuffles every 10s — tap to reshuffle now

graph.cap: Trade consistency vs availability under network partitions — choose per system.
graph.sharding: Split data across shards to scale writes/reads beyond one node.
graph.saga: Long transactions across services via compensating steps, no 2PC.
graph.idempotency: Same result no matter how many times a call retries.
graph.redlock: Distributed lock on Redis + fencing token to stop client races.
graph.kafka: Append-only log as source of truth: replay, decouple producers/consumers.
graph.cdc: Capture DB changes (Debezium) to a stream — no dual writes.
graph.token-bucket: Smooth rate limiting with token buckets + atomic Redis Lua.
graph.websocket: Two-way realtime gateway, fan-out events to thousands of clients.
graph.tracing: Follow one request across services to find bottlenecks (OTel/Jaeger).
graph.nestjs: Modular backend framework with clean DI for large APIs.
graph.typeorm: TypeScript ORM: entities, relations, migrations — close to SQL.
graph.webhooks: Receive third-party events safely: verify signature + idempotent.
graph.payment: Integrate payment gateways: idempotency, reconciliation, refunds.
graph.rag: Semantic search with pgvector, then grounded LLM answers with sources.
graph.oauth: Auth and authorization via OAuth2/JWT with safe refresh tokens.
graph.jobs: Background job queues: retry, backoff, scheduling — off the request path.
graph.rsc: React Server Components: render on the server, ship less JS.
graph.k8s: Container orchestration: self-healing, scaling, declarative rollouts.
graph.ingress: Route HTTP into the cluster: host/path, TLS, load balancing.
graph.argocd: GitOps: desired state in Git, ArgoCD syncs it to the cluster.
graph.rollouts: Canary/blue-green deploys that analyze metrics before full traffic.
graph.prometheus: Collect metrics and alert in real time for running systems.
graph.rbac: Role-based access in K8s: who can do what, least privilege.
graph.terraform: Infrastructure as code, multi-cloud, reproducible plan/apply.
graph.falco: Detect abnormal container runtime behavior in real time.
graph.consistentHashing: Consistent hashing: add/remove nodes with minimal re-keying — the base for elastic sharding.
graph.circuitBreaker: Trip the circuit when a dependency keeps failing — stop cascading failures.
graph.eventSourcing: Store the event stream as source of truth; rebuild state by replay.
graph.readReplica: Read-only replicas absorb read load — at the cost of replication lag.
graph.graphql: Typed API: clients fetch exactly the fields they need from one endpoint.
graph.redisCache: Cache-aside on Redis: TTL, invalidation, stampede protection.
graph.sse: Server-Sent Events: lightweight one-way server push for streams.
graph.zodValidation: Runtime validation + inferred types with Zod at the API edge.
graph.helm: Package K8s manifests into reusable charts with per-env values.
graph.autoscaling: HPA scales pods by CPU/custom metrics — tracks real load.
graph.serviceMesh: A mesh layer handling mTLS, retries, and traffic-splitting between services.
graph.grafana: Visual dashboards + alerts on top of Prometheus metrics.
